overview: This Legacy Mono-White Stax archetype leverages artifact mana sources like Mox Diamond and Grim Monolith to accelerate into Trinisphere. By restricting opponent spells to three mana or less, the deck stifles fast strategies and transitions into a grindy control game. The strategy relies on mana denial and incremental white disruption to outlast opponents in the late game.
win_conditions: Deploying late-game white threats after opponent is stalled
Winning via artifact synergy and card advantage

keep_criteria: Hands with Mox Diamond or Grim Monolith
Hands with early white stax effects
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetches if applicable), plus at least one tax effect (Trinisphere, Thalia, Winter Orb) and a mana source (Mox Diamond, Grim Monolith, or basic lands).
Prioritize hands that can establish a tax floor by turn 2-3.
mulligan_criteria: Hands heavy on late-game cards without setup
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only expensive spells without acceleration, or hands missing both tax effects and mana rocks.

early_game: Play Mox Diamond or Grim Monolith on turn one
Cast Trinisphere as soon as possible to restrict opponent spells
Use early white stax to delay aggressive starts
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Mox Diamond or Grim Monolith if mana allows.
Turn 2: Drop Trinisphere or Thalia if available
If you have Grim Monolith, consider tapping it for mana on turn 2 to cast a second tax effect or a disruption piece.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast your tax effects by turn 2-3 without stalling.
mid_game: Cycle through white disruption to counter key threats
Develop board presence while opponent struggles to cast spells
Mana is your most valuable resource.
Use Grim Monolith strategically to cast multiple tax effects or disruption in a single turn.
Mox Diamond requires careful sequencing to avoid dead mana.
Keep cards in hand to respond to key threats, but don't hoard if you can stabilize.
Graveyard is a resource for you (via fetch lands or recursion) but a liability for opponents (Rest in Peace).
Commit threats only when you have established a tax floor.
late_game: Leverage mana advantage to outvalue opponent
Close out games by exhausting opponent's resources
Out-grinding via tax effects, or using Knight of the Reliquary to fetch a land for mana, or casting a large spell with Grim Monolith.
Sometimes a resilient creature like Knight of the Reliquary or a win con like Empty the Warrens (if in the list) closes it out.
Go all-in when you've established a permanent tax floor (Trinisphere + Thalia/Winter Orb) and the opponent has no way to remove your key pieces.

key_cards.name: Mox Diamond
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to enable early Trinisphere and smooth out mana curve

key_cards.name: Grim Monolith
key_cards.notes: Provides consistent mana to cast white stax spells and keep Trinisphere relevant

key_cards.name: Trinisphere
key_cards.notes: Forces opponents to pay three mana for all spells, crippling fast decks and enabling control game plan

sequencing_tips: Always play artifact mana before Trinisphere to ensure it can be cast
Hold up white mana for stax effects when opponent has cheap threats
Prioritize keeping Trinisphere protected from artifact hate
Sequence disruption to maximize opponent's mana inefficiency
Stax is a fragile deck if your key pieces are removed.
Protect Trinisphere and Thalia at all costs.
Mox Diamond's sacrifice cost can be a liability in slow games.
Grim Monolith requires careful mana management.

matchups.advice: Play Trinisphere immediately to stop cheap threats and force inefficient mana usage
Race with stax effects and leverage Trinisphere to slow their setup
Restrict mana early and use white stax to disrupt key combo pieces
Use cheap, multi-target tax effects. Cast spells in response to counterspells if possible, or use mana rocks to cast around them. Trinisphere makes counterspells expensive.
Rest in Peace, Wasteland, and Winter Orb are key. Tax their mana with Trinisphere/Thalia. Don't let them establish a mana base.
It's a race to establish tax effects. Prioritize keeping Trinisphere and Thalia. Use Wasteland to disrupt their mana. Win by having more resilient artifacts or better sequencing.

common_threats: Watch for mana acceleration (Tron, Sneak and Show), card draw engines (Dimir Tempo, Ocelot Pride), and graveyard recursion.
Key tells: Opponent tapping extra lands, casting spells from exile/graveyard, or holding up mana for counterspells.
